name: Dip and Sprinkle
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift already sifted flour with salt and set aside.
2. In a large bowl, cream butter with an electric mixer at medium speed. Add sugar, egg yolk, and vanilla. Beat until light and fluffy. Gradually add the flour and salt mixture and almonds, mixing until well blended.
3. Shape dough into two logs, each 1 1/2 inches in diameter, and wrap in wax paper. Refrigerate until firm, at least two hours.
4. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Lightly grease a cookie sheet. Using a ruler, mark each log at 1/4 inch intervals. With a sharp thin knife, cut into slices and place onto the prepared cookie sheet, one inch apart.
6. Bake for 8-10 minutes or until lightly browned. Remove from the cookie sheet and cool on a rack.
7. Prepare chocolate mixture by melting chocolate chips and butter in the top of a double boiler over hot water. Stir in hot water.
8. Lay a sheet of wax paper on a table. Dip half of each cookie into the hot chocolate mixture and place onto the wax paper.
9. If using sprinkles, dip a few cookies and then sprinkle while the chocolate is still wet. Let dry for at least one hour.
